Study Hub / Pak Study / Testpoint / Lesson 11

Lesson 11

Questions 2001–2200 · Tap a card to reveal the explanation.

1

As per 2023 census, what is the total population of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: 241 million
Show explanation
Explanation

2023 national census (released on 5 August 2023)

Growth rate: 2.55 per cent

Total Pakistan population: 241.49 million

Punjab: 127.68 million

Punjab growth rate: 2.53 percent

Sindh population: 55.69 million

Sindh growth rate: 2.57 percent

Balochistan population: 14.89 million

Balochistan growth rate: 3.2 percent (highest of all)

KPK population: 40.85 million

KPK growth rate: 2.38 percent

Pakistan rural population: 61.18 percent

Pakistan Urban population: 38.82 percent

33 million population increased between 2017 and 2023 

110 million population increased between 1998 and 2023 

*****

2

Shahid Khaqan Abbssi was the ______ Prime Minister of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: 21st
Show explanation
Explanation

Shahid Khaqan Abbasi is a Pakistani politician

who served as the 21st prime minister of Pakistan

from August 2017 to May 2018.

3

Which birth anniversary Quaid-i-Azam Mohammad Ali Jinnah was celebrated in 2020?

Correct Answer: 144th
Show explanation
Explanation

Nation celebrates Quaid's 144th birth anniversary amid messages of 'hope and courage' from leaders.

Mr Jinnah was born on December 25, 1876, in Karachi. 


4

Who was the Chief Minister of Punjab in 2017?

Correct Answer: Shahbaz Sharif
Show explanation
Explanation

Shahbaz Sharif served as the Chief Minister of Punjab from 2013 to 2018.

His tenure focused on infrastructure development and governance reforms.

5

The current motto of Punjab Police is _______?

Correct Answer: To Serve and Protect
Show explanation
Explanation

The motto of the Punjab Police in Pakistan is "To Serve and Protect Our Punjab Always Stand With People". 

The Punjab Police was formed on March 22, 1861.

It has 350,000 employees.

The Punjab Police has several mobile apps, including: Crime Prevention App, Personnel Mobile App, Tourist Facilitation App, and Zindagi App.

6

Who can approve the detention of a person under Federal law (Article 10)?

Correct Answer: Chief Justice of Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation

Under Article 10, a person detained under Federal law requires the approval or review of the Chief Justice of Pakistan.

This ensures judicial oversight over preventive detention at the federal level.

7

Under which article of the 1973 Constitution any law inconsistent with or in derogation of Fundamental Rights to be void?

Correct Answer: Article 8
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 8 of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan states that any law inconsistent with or in derogation of Fundamental Rights will be considered void.

This ensures the protection of citizens' fundamental rights under the Constitution.

8

Pakistan's Constitution provides protection against retrospective punishment _____?

Correct Answer: Yes
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan's Constitution does provide protection against retrospective punishment under Article 12.

It states that no law shall authorize punishment for an act that was not punishable when committed.

9

Article 24 of the Constitution of Pakistan deals with ______?

Correct Answer: Protection of property
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 24 ensures that no person shall be deprived of their property except in accordance with the law.

It provides legal protection and safeguards against arbitrary seizure of property by the state.

10

Article 6 of the constitution of Pakistan 1973 deals with _____?

Correct Answer: High treason
Show explanation
Explanation

High Treason means سنگین غداری

 Any person who abrogates or subverts or suspends or holds in abeyance, or attempts or conspires to abrogate or subvert or suspend or hold in abeyance, the Constitution by use of force or show of force or by any other unconstitutional means shall be guilty of high treason.

************

FIA CBT VU PAPER 17-12-2021

11

Article 73(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a money bill shall be originated in _____?

Correct Answer: The National Assembly
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 73(1) of the Constitution 1973 states that a Money Bill can only originate in the National Assembly.

This maintains the principle that financial control rests with directly elected representatives of the people.

12

Which article of the Constitution of Pakistan obligates the state to provide free and compulsory education to children of the age group 5 to 16 years?

Correct Answer: 25-A
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 25-A of the Constitution of Pakistan obligates the state to provide free and compulsory education to children aged 5 to 16 years.

This article ensures that every child has access to education as a fundamental right.

13

Election Commission is formed under which Article?

Correct Answer: Article 218
Show explanation
Explanation

The Election Commission of Pakistan is established under Article 218 of the Constitution.

This Article ensures free and fair elections in Pakistan.

14

The Chairman of Council of Common Interest is the _____?

Correct Answer: Prime Minister
Show explanation
Explanation

The Prime Minister of Pakistan is the Chairman of the Council of Common Interests (CCI).

The CCI is a constitutional body established under Article 153 of the 1973 Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

The CCI's other members are:

  • The four Provincial Chief Ministers
  • Three members nominated by the Prime Minister, usually Cabinet members
15

Name the deputy chief of TTP killed by Pakistani forces in October 2025?

Correct Answer: Amjad alias Mazahim
Show explanation
Explanation

On 30 October 2025, Pakistani security forces killed 22 terrorists, including the banned Tehreek-i-Taliban Pakistan’s (TTP) second-in-command, in three separate operations in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Balochistan.

Four terrorists, including the “high-value target” Amjad alias Mazahim, were killed.

 

 

Additional Info:

According to ISPR, Amjad was a deputy to TTP chief Noor Wali Mehsud and headed the group’s Rehbari Shura.

He was wanted by law enforcement agencies and carried a Rs5 million bounty.

****

16

Which two countries mediated ceasefire between Pakistan and Afghanistan in October 2025?

Correct Answer: Turkiye and Qatar
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan and Afghanistan agreed to continue a ceasefire between the two countries following a rapid deterioration of ties and a brief conflict along their shared border.

Truce extended on 31 October 2025.

Mediated by Turkiye and Qatar

***

17

Moenjodaro is in which province of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

Mohenjo-Daro is Located in Larkana upper Sindh

It was the largest settlement of the ancient Indus Valley Civilisation

it was first recognized in 1922

R. D. Banerji discovered it

Mohenjo-Daro means Mound of the Dead

This is because the remains started to have layers over them after they had declined.

18

What is the current constitution of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: 1973 Constitution
Show explanation
Explanation

The current constitution of Pakistan is the 1973 Constitution.

It was adopted on August 14, 1973.

It serves as the fundamental legal document governing the country.

19

Dina Wadia was the daughter of Muhammad Ali Jinnah was born on ______?

Correct Answer: 15 August 1919
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Dina Wadia was the daughter of Muhammad Ali Jinnah
    Born: August 15, 1919, London, United Kingdom
    Died: November 2, 2017, New York, New York, United States
    Nationality: American, British
    ***********
    • Mohammad Ali Jinnah Born: December 25, 1876, Karachi
    • Pakistan's first governor-general until his death
    • Mohammed Ali Jinnah died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi, Pakistan.
    • Mohammed Ali Jinnah was the founder and first governor-general (1947–48) of Pakistan
    • Spouse: Rattanbai Jinnah (m. 1918–1929), Emibai Jinnah (m. 1892–1893)
    • Siblings: Fatima Jinnah, Shireen Jinnah, Maryam Bai Jinnah, Ahmad Ali Jinnah, Bunde Ali Jinnah, Rahmat Ali Jinnah
    • Children: Dina Wadia
    • Parents: Poonja Jinnah, Mithibai Jinnah

20

Who has been declared Sadiq and Ameen by Supreme Court of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Imran Khan
Show explanation
Explanation
Imran Khan was declared Sadiq and Ameen by the Supreme Court of Pakistan in December 2017.
The verdict affirmed his honesty and integrity under Article 62(1)(f) of the Constitution.
21

When was the Metro Bus Lahore launched?

Correct Answer: 2013
Show explanation
Explanation

Lahore Metro Bus was inaugurated on February 10, 2013.

Construction of the project began in March 2012.

It was launched by the Government of Punjab to provide affordable urban transport.

22

How many divisions are there in Punjab, Pakistan?

Correct Answer: 12
Show explanation
Explanation

NOTE: There are 12 divisions in Pakistan; this information is not updated on any website ..... SO don't be confused, 12 division is the right question 


List of 10 Divisions of Punjab

  • - Lahore  
  • - Gujranwala  
  • - Faisalabad  
  • - Sargodha  
  • - Rawalpindi  
  • - Sahiwal  
  • - Multan  
  • - Bahawalpur  
  • - Dera Ghazi Khan  
  • - Gujrat
  • Mianwali
  • Murree
23

Which of the following is first woman to become Chief Minister of any province of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Maryam Nawaz
Show explanation
Explanation

Maryam Nawaz Sharif is currently serving as the Chief Minister of Punjab on 26 February 2024

Predecessor: Moshin Naqvi

Party Name: PML-N

Note: Maryam Nawaz is the first woman to become Chief Minister of any province of Pakistan.

24

Which type of leave Federal Government employees cannot take?

Correct Answer: Casual Leave without reason
Show explanation
Explanation

Federal Government employees cannot take casual leave without a valid reason or prior approval.

Every leave type—casual, earned, or medical—requires justification or sanction under Revised Leave Rules, 1980.


More Details
Casual leave is only for short emergencies.
Unauthorized absence is treated as Leave without Pay (LWP).

وفاقی ملازمین بغیر کسی وجہ یا منظوری کے کیشول چھٹی نہیں لے سکتے، ایسی غیر حاضری بغیر تنخواہ شمار ہوتی ہے

25

Federal Shariat Court employees casual leave for one year is:

Correct Answer: 20 days
Show explanation
Explanation

Employees of the Federal Shariat Court are entitled to 20 days of casual leave per calendar year, as per Federal Government Leave Rules.


More Details
Casual leave cannot be combined with earned leave.
Granted only for personal or urgent matters.

وفاقی شرعی عدالت کے ملازمین کو وفاقی حکومت کے رولز کے مطابق سالانہ 20 دن کی چھٹی ملتی ہے۔

26

The total number of members of the Federal Shariat Court are ______?

Correct Answer: 8 members
Show explanation
Explanation

Federal Shariat Court of Pakistan has 8 judges, 3 are required to be Ulema.

The judges hold office for a period of 3 years (extended by the President).

Created by Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq in 1980.

27

Who appoints the Judges of the Federal Shariat Court?

Correct Answer: President of Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation

Judges of the Federal Shariat Court (FSC) are appointed by the President of Pakistan under Article 203C of the Constitution.

More Details

  • The Court consists of 8 Muslim Judges, including the Chief Justice.

  • The President consults with the Chief Justice of Pakistan before appointment.


وفاقی شرعی عدالت کے ججوں کو پاکستان کے صدر آئین کے آرٹیکل 203C کے تحت مقرر کرتے ہیں۔

28

Gilgit Baltistan is a _____?

Correct Answer: Administrative territory of Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation

Gilgit is a city of: Gilgit-Baltistan Pakistan

Gilgit Baltistan is located on the North side of Pakistan

Gilgit-Baltistan, formerly known as the Northern Areas, is a region administered by Pakistan as an administrative territory.

Gilgit-Baltistan was Established on 1 November 1948

Capital of Gilgit-Baltistan is Gilgit

Largest city of Gilgit-Baltistan is Skardu

    agm19.3.23

    Fatima Jinnah is known as _______?

    Correct Answer: Madr-e-Millat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fatima Jinnah: younger sister of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah

    Fatima Jinnah born: 31th July 1893

    Fatima Jinnah is referred as:  Madr-e-Millat (Mother of Nation)

    Fatima Jinnah died: 9 July 1967

    Fatima Jinnah died at age: 71

    Fatima Jinnah died: Karachi

    Fatima Jinnah profession: Dental Surgeon

    30

    دریائے سندھ دنیا کا سب سے بڑا دریا ہے۔

    Correct Answer: غلط
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    دریائے سندھ پاکستان کا سب سے لمبا دریا ہے۔  

    دنیا کا سب سے لمبا دریا نیل  ہے

    31

    قرارداد پاکستان کب منظور ہوئی؟

    Correct Answer: 1940
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    بائیس مارچ سے 24 مارچ، 1940ء کو لاہور کے منٹو پارک میں آل انڈیا مسلم لیگ کے تین روزہ سالانہ اجلاس کے اختتام پر وہ تاریخی قرارداد منظور کی گئی تھی

    جس کی بنیاد پر مسلم لیگ نے برصغیر میں مسلمانوں کے لیے عليحدہ

    وطن کے حصول کے لیے تحریک شروع کی اور سات برس کے بعد اپنا مطالبہ منظور کرانے میں کامیاب رہی



    32

    Quaid-e-Azam Mausoleum is located in which city?

    Correct Answer: Karachi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Quaid e Azam Mausoleum is located in Karachi.

    Mazar-e-Quaid, also known as Jinnah Mausoleum or the National Mausoleum

    Mazar-e-Quaid is the final resting place of Muhammad Ali Jinnah, the founder of Pakistan.

    Designed in a 1960s modernist style,

    it was completed and open on June 2, 1970

    Architect: Yahya Merchant

    Mazar-e-Quaid is an iconic symbol of Karachi

    while 

    Quaid-e-Azam Residency is situated in Ziarat.

    33

    Who was the final authority to approve Constitutional amendments?

    Correct Answer: The President
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    For constitutional amendments, the final authority to grant assent is the President.

    However, the process first requires approval from both the Senate and the National Assembly. 


    34

    Decisions of Council of Common Interest are binding on?

    Correct Answer: Federation and Provinces
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Federal Government:
    The federal government is required to implement the decisions of the CCI.
    Provincial Governments:
    The council's decisions bind all provinces.
    Related Institutions:
    Institutions that fall under the purview of Part II of the Federal Legislative List are also under the supervision and control of the CCI and must adhere to its policies
    35

    Who nominates the Chief of Army Staff?

    Correct Answer: Prime Minister
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Prime Minister approves the nomination and appointment of the Chief of Army Staff

    The Chief of the Army Staff (COAS) is a position in the Pakistani Army typically held by a four-star general.

    Who was the founder of Dar-Ul-Uloom Deoband institute?

    Correct Answer: Muhammad Qasim Nanautavi

    Founded: 31 May 1866

    Founder: Muhammad Qasim Nanautavi

    First Principal: Maulana Muhammad Qasim

    Location: Saharanpur, Uttar Pardesh

    Correct Answer: Muhammad Tahir Rai
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muhammad Tahir, a Grade-21 officer, was previously serving in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

    38

    What is the name of first Hyperspectral Satellite launched by Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: HS-1
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan launched its first Hyperspectral Satellite  from a Chinese launch centre

    It was launched by Pakistani space agency SUPARCO

    Launched on 19 October 2025.

    It has been named as “HS-1”

     

    Additional Information:

    Hyperspectral imaging is a type of advanced camera technology used in satellites to study the Earth and space.

    39

    Renowned Sindhi writer Muhammad Ibrahim Joyo buried in _____?

    Correct Answer: Jamshoro
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muhammad Ibrahim Joyo passed away on 9 November 2017 at the age of 102.

    He died at his elder son’s residence in Hyderabad, Sindh, Pakistan.

    He was buried at the Allama I.I. Kazi Mausoleum located in Sindh University, Jamshoro.

    40

    Where is Warsak Dam situated in which province?

    Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Warsak Dam is located on the Kabul River in the Valley of Peshawar

    Warsak Dam is built on the Kabul river

    Construction began 1949

    it was Opened in 1960

    the Height of dam is 76 m

    Its length is 140.2 m (460 ft)

    Warsak Dam was completed under the Colombo Plan in two phases 

    it was financed by the Canadian Government.

    41

    Who among the following was born in the 17th century? Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai, Sachal Sarmast, Mirza Ghalib, None of these

    Correct Answer: Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai, was a Sindhi Sufi mystic, and poet.

    He is widely considered to be the greatest poet of the Sindhi language. 

    Born: November 18, 1689 (17th century), Hala

    Died: January 1, 1752, Bhit Shah

    42

    Pakistan has awarded the rank of Field Marshal to ______?

    Correct Answer: Both (A) and (B)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 20 May 2025, the federal cabinet approved the promotion of Chief of Army Staff (COAS) General Asim Munir to Field Marshal.

    For ensuring the security of the country and defeating the enemy based on the high strategy and courageous leadership of the Battle of Truth, Operation Bunyan-um-Marsoos.


    In 1965, Ayub Khan appointed himself as Field Marshal of Pakistan.

    He was the first and the only Field Marshall of the Pakistani Army,

    Field Marshal of the Pakistan Army has five stars.

    Ayub Khan forced Iskander Mirza to resign and assumed power of president on 27th October 1958

    Ayub Khan also served in the civilian government as Defence and Home Minister from 1953 to 1958.

    He assumed the Presidency after exiling President Iskandar Mirza in 1958.

    43

    The test of atom bomb of Pakistan was conducted at ______?

    Correct Answer: Chagai
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

    The Pakistani Atomic Energy Commission reported that the five nuclear tests conducted on May 28, 1998.

    44

    In which year Pakistan launched its first satellite?

    Correct Answer: 1990
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan launched its first satellite, Badr-1, on July 16, 1990,

    Launch Vehicle: Chinese Long March 2E rocket.

    End of mission

    Last contact: 21 August 1990

    Decay date : 9 December 1990


    45

    In Northern Kingdom Gandhara Kingdom developed around

    Correct Answer: BC 4th Century BC
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Gandhara Civilization flourished in Pakistan during the rule of Buddhist Kings.

    • Taxila was considered as Pearl of Gandhara Civilization. 
    • language of the Gandhara civilization was:  Prakrit
    • ***
    • Gandhara's language was a Prakrit or "Middle Indo-Aryan" dialect, usually called Gāndhārī.
    • Hindko from Peshawar, which was the capital of Gandhara, came from Shuraseni Prakrit a language spoken in Gandhara.


    46

    When first satellite Badar-1 was launched into space?

    Correct Answer: 16 July 1990
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • On July 16, 1990, Pakistan launched its first satellite, Badr-1 (also known as PRSS-1), into space.
      • Pakistan becoming the first Islamic country to venture into space exploration.
      • It was launched as a secondary payload on a Long March 2E rocket from the Xichang Satellite Launch Center (XSLC) in China.
      47

      Which river is the main river flowing through Khyber Pakhtunkhwa?

      Correct Answer: Indus
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Indus River is the main river flowing through Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK), a province in Pakistan.

      The Indus River originates in Tibet and flows through India and Pakistan.

      It passing through the KPK province before entering Punjab province and eventually draining into the Arabian Sea.

      48

      What is the National sport of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Hockey
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup with four championships:

      1.       1971,

      2.       1978,

      3.       1982

      4.       1994

      49

      Independent Power Producers (IPPs) were introduced during which government?

      Correct Answer: PPP Government
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Independent Power Producers (IPPs) were first introduced in Pakistan during the government of Benazir Bhutto in 1994.

      50

      Where is the famous Badsahahi mosque situated?

      Correct Answer: Lahore
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Badshahi Masjid in Lahore is also called Emperor mosque

      Badshahi Masjid at Lahore 3rd the largest mosque

      The Badshahi Mosque was built by Emperor Aurangzeb in 1671,

      Construction of Badshahi Mosque in Lahore completed in:1673

      In 1947, Pakistan uplifted the Badshahi Mosque and citadel Lahore as symbols of the state.

      Architect: Nawab Zain Yar Jang Bahadur

      Opened: 1673

      Height: 69 m

      Number of minarets: 8


      ND13-1-2023
      51

      Name the Pakistani submarine which was action in 1965 and 1971 wars. It sank in 1971 war while on a mission?

      Correct Answer: PNS Ghazi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      PNS Ghazi was a Pakistani submarine that played a significant role in both the 1965 and 1971 wars with India.

      Unfortunately, it sank on December 3, 1971, while on a mission to attack the Indian aircraft carrier INS Vikrant during the Indo-Pakistani War of 1971.

      52

      Who among the following was the last ruler of Delhi Sultanate?

      Correct Answer: Ibrahim Lodi
      Show explanation
      Explanation


      The Lodhi Dynasty was the last dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

      It was ruling from 1451 until the defeat by Babur in 1526, which led to the establishment of the Mughal Empire.

      The first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate was the Mamluk Dynasty (also known as the Slave Dynasty), which began with Qutb al-Din Aibak in 1206.

      53

      The nationalization policy was introduced by ______?

      Correct Answer: Z.A. Bhutto
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Zulfikar Ali Bhutto (PPP) announced the nationalization of major industries on January 2, 1972.

      These industries included iron and steel, heavy engineering, heavy electricals, petrochemicals, cement, and public utilities.

      54

      Asif Khan is a famous Pakistani player of which sport?

      Correct Answer: Cricket
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Asif Khan, born in Lahore in 1990.

      He is a right-handed batsman and right-arm off-break bowler.

      He played domestic cricket in Pakistan before moving to the United Arab Emirates in 2017 to potentially play for their national team.

      He later made his One Day International (ODI) debut for the UAE in 2022.

      His Twenty20 International (T20I) debut in 2023. 

      55

      Who was the founder of "Khaksar Tehreek"?

      Correct Answer: Allama Inayatullah Mashraqi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Khaksar Movement was a social movement based in Lahore, Punjab, British India.

      It was established by Allama Inayatullah Mashraqi in 1931.

      The movement aimed to free India from British rule.

      Allama Mashriqi disbanded the Khaksar Tehrik on July 4, 1947.

      56

      M5 motorway connects which cities?

      Correct Answer: Multan - Sukkar
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Motorway M5 (Multan-Sukkur section) connects Sukkur in Pakistan's Sindh province and Multan in the Punjab region

      • Length 392 km (244 mi)
      • Existed 2018–present

      ****History*****

      • The motorway was constructed during Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif's rule
      • The first motorway project was Completed in 1999 the six-lane Lahore to Islamabad Motorway,
      • now this motorway called the M-2 was the first highway of in Pakistan.

      RK4-12-22

      57

      Pakistan won its first Hockey World Cup in 1971 by defeating which of the following in the final?

      Correct Answer: Spain
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On 2 August 1948, Pakistan national team, led by Ali Iqtidar Shah Dara, officially went on to play their first international game and tournament against Belgium winning the game 2–0 at the 1948 London Olympics.

      Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup.

       Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

      1971

      1978

      1982

      1994.

      ***

      Pakistan defeats Spain in the 1971 hockey world cup.

      Pakistan beat India to secure the bronze medal at Hockey Champions Trophy 2012.


      Pakistan played its first International Hockey matches in 1948

      In 1994 year, Pakistan was the world champion of Hockey

      In 1994 en Pakistan won the World Hockey Cup last time


      58

      To revitalize Khilafat which movement launched?

      Correct Answer: Khilafat movement
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched to support the Khilafat Movement.

      The Khilafat Movement was a political campaign that took place in British India from 1919–1924.

      The movement's goal was to preserve the Ottoman Caliphate after World War I and to influence the treaty-making process.

      The Non-Cooperation Movement was a wider movement that Mahatma Gandhi advocated for in support of the Khilafat Movement.

      The movement lost momentum after the caliphate was abolished in 1924.

      The Ottoman Empire officially ceased to exist in 1923 when Turkey became a republic.

      59

      Faith, Unity and Discipline is the motto of _____________?

      Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      When the Quaid gave us this legacy, what he meant was ‘unity’ within our ranks, ‘faith’ in this nation and the destiny and will of God Almighty, and ‘discipline’ with total commitment to Pakistan.

      جب قائد نے ہمیں یہ ورثہ دیا تو ان کا مطلب یہ تھا کہ ہماری صفوں میں ’اتحاد‘، اس قوم پر ’ایمان‘ اور اللہ تعالیٰ کی تقدیر اور مرضی، اور ’نظم و ضبط‘ پاکستان سے مکمل وابستگی ہے۔

      ****

      Faith, Unity, Discipline (ایمان، اتحاد، نظم) is the national motto of Pakistan.
      It is regarded as the guiding principle of Pakistan's nationhood.


      60

      Prime Minister Imran Khan has laid the foundation stone of Central Business District (CBD) in ________?

      Correct Answer: Lahore
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Prime Minister Imran Khan on February 26, 2021 laid the foundation stone of the ambitious Central Business District (CBD) project at the Walton Airport site, asserting that the project would help create much-needed wealth to pay off foreign loans that had accumulated over the past ‘decade of darkness’.

      61

      Humayun was defeated by Sher Shah Suri in which battle?

      Correct Answer: Battle of Kannauj
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Humayun, the Mughal emperor, was defeated by Sher Shah Suri in the Battle of Kannauj (1540).

      This defeat led to Humayun’s temporary exile and Sher Shah Suri establishing the Suri dynasty.

      62

      Pervez Hoodbhoy was a _____?

      Correct Answer: Nuclear Physicist and Educationist
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pervez Hoodbhoy is a Pakistani nuclear physicist who holds a Ph.D. in nuclear physics from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).

      He has taught physics for many years at Quaid-e-Azam University and other institutions, such as Forman Christian College University, establishing him as a prominent educationist.

      63

      Pakistan lost the cricket world cup final in which year?

      Correct Answer: 1999
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan lost the 1999 Cricket World Cup final to Australia at Lord's, London.

      Australia won by 8 wickets, bowling Pakistan out for 132 runs.

      64

      Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan was a scientist of _____?

      Correct Answer: Nuclear / Metallurgical Program (Atomic Program of Pakistan)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan earned his Ph.D. from the Catholic University of Leuven in Belgium.

      He completed his doctoral studies in metallurgical engineering in 1972

       Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan is widely regarded as the "Saviour of Pakistan"

      "Mohsin-e-Pakistan" (Savior of Pakistan).

      He is colloquially known as the father of Pakistan's atomic weapons program.


      Science Advisor to the Presidential Secretariat:In office 1 January 2001 – 31 January 2004

      Born: 1 April 1936, Bhopal, Bhopal State, India

      Died: 10 October 2021 (aged 85) Islamabad

      Political party: Tehreek-e-Tahaffuz-e-Pakistan (2012–2013)

      Awards

      • Nishan-i-Imtiaz (1996; 1999)
      • Hilal-i-Imtiaz (1989)
      65

      The Green Line Railway is part of which project?

      Correct Answer: CPEC Improvement Plan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Green Line Express is a high-speed rail project under the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC).

      It aims to modernize Pakistan’s railway system and reduce travel time between Karachi and Lahore.

      66

      Pakistan won the Hockey World Cup in 1994 against which team in the final?

      Correct Answer: Netherlands
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On 2 August 1948, Pakistan national team, led by Ali Iqtidar Shah Dara, officially went on to play their first international game and tournament against Belgium winning the game 2–0 at the 1948 London Olympics.

      Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup.

       Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

      1971

      1978

      1982

      1994.

      ***

      Pakistan defeats Spain in the 1971 hockey world cup.

      Pakistan beat India to secure the bronze medal at Hockey Champions Trophy 2012.


      Pakistan played its first International Hockey matches in 1948

      In 1994 year, Pakistan was the world champion of Hockey

      In 1994 Pakistan won the World Hockey Cup last time

      67

      Muhammad Ali Bogra formula was about _____?

      Correct Answer: Equal representation
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Bogra Formula was a political compromise presented and proposed by Prime Minister Bogra on 7 October 1953

      The Bogra Formula sought to permanently abolish the Governor-General and  to be replaced with the elected figurehead presidency.

      68

      Hashoo Group and Pearl Continental Hotels are owned by ______?

      Correct Answer: Sadruddin Hashwani
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sadruddin Hashwani is a prominent Pakistani businessman.

      He owns the Hashoo Group and the famous Pearl Continental Hotels chain in Pakistan.

      69

      Kunchi Saar Peak is found in _____?

      Correct Answer: Hindu Kush Range
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Kunchi Saar Peak is located in the Hindu Kush mountain range.

      The Hindu Kush stretches across Afghanistan and northern Pakistan.

      70

      The aim of Objectives Resolution was to ______?

      Correct Answer: Frame the constitution on the basis of Islamic concept of the sovereignty of God
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Objectives Resolution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan on March 12, 1949.

      Prime Minister, Liaquat Ali Khan, had presented it in the assembly on March 7, 1949.

      Out of 75 members of the assembly, 21 voted for it. All the amendments proposed by minority members were rejected.

      Consequently, all ten of them voted against

      The resolution, in its entirety, has been made part of the Constitution of Pakistan under Article 2(A)

      ...................

      Quaid - i - Azam is the first President of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan

      Liaquat Ali Khan headed after the death of Jinnah for three years and produced the Objectives Resolution.

      ............

      During 5 meetings of the First Constituent Assembly, the Objective Resolution was passed

      ..............

      The aim of the objective resolution was to Frame the constitution on the basis of the Islamic concept of the sovereignty of God

      ...................

      The members of the constituent assembly was increased from 69 to 79

      M6 is the motorway between ________?

      Correct Answer: Hyderabad and Sukkur
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On 13 December 2022, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif laid the foundation for the 306 km Sukkur-Hyderabad section of M6.
      This project is part of Pakistan’s national motorway network to improve connectivity and traffic flow in the region.

      The Gaddani Beach is located in ?

      Jahangir​

      75

      پاکستان میں رائج الوقت قوانین کو اسلامی ڈھانچے میں ڈھالنے کےلیے کون سا ادارہ قائم کیا گیا ہے؟

      Correct Answer: اسلامی نظریاتی کونسل
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      اسلامی نظریاتی کونسل، پاکستان کا آئینی ادارہ ہے۔ 1973ء کے آئین میں جب شق نمبر 227 شامل کی گئی

      جس کے مطابق پاکستان میں کوئی بھی قانون قرآن وسنت کے مخالف نہیں بنایا جائے گا تو عملاً اس کا باقاعدہ نظام وضع کرنے کی غرض سے

      اسی آئین میں دفعہ نمبر 228، 229 اور 230 میں اسلامی نظریاتی کونسل کے نام سے 20 اراکین پر مشتمل ایک آئینی ادارہ بھی تشکیل دیا گیا

      جس کا مقصد صدر، گورنر یا اسمبلی کی اکثریت کی طرف سے بھیجے جانے والے معاملے کی اسلامی حیثیت کا جائزہ لے کر 15 دنوں کے اندر اندر انھیں اپنی رپورٹ پیش کرنا تھا۔ شق نمبر 228 میں یہ قرار دیا گیا

      کہ اس کے اراکین میں جہاں تمام فقہی مکاتب ِفکر کی مساوی نمائندگی ضروری ہوگی وہاں اس کے کم از کم چار ارکان ایسے ہوں گے جنھوں نے اسلامی تعلیم و تحقیق میں کم وبیش 15 برس لگائے ہو اور انھیں جمہورِ پاکستان کا اعتماد حاصل ہو۔


      76

      Which of following not among the fundamental rights of citizens ?

      Correct Answer: Freedom of education
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Article 8-28 of 1973 constitution of Pakistan  deals  with  fundamental rights 

      77

      Juvenile prisoners are under the age of ____?

      Correct Answer: 18 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Juvenile prisoners are generally under the age of 18 years.

      The term "juvenile" refers to individuals who have not yet reached adulthood.

      They are subject to different legal and correctional standards.

      78

      Loraalai District is located in which Province of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Balochistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       It is located in the northeastern part of Balochistan province of Pakistan.

      It is also known as Bori (Pashto: بورۍ)

      It is 4,700 feet (1,400 m) above sea level. 

      Loralai District has the following Tehsils in it:

      • Bori , also known as Loralai
      • Duki
      79

      Gwadar port is in the province of ______?

      Correct Answer: Balochistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in the Balochistan province of Pakistan

      The Gwadar Port was Opened: in 2002;

      Pakistan purchased Gwadar port from Oman on 8th September 1958.

      Gwadar officially became part of Pakistan on 8th December 1958.

      Gwadar remained under Omani rule for 174 years from 1783 -1958 

      Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in Balochistan province.

      Opened: 2002 

      while 

      Pakistan has three big sea ports  It was purchased from Oman in 1958.

      Karachi Port,

      Muhammad Bin Qasim port

      and Gwadar port.

      The sale of cigarettes is prohibited in Pakistan to persons under the age of _____?

      Correct Answer: 18
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The sale of cigarettes and tobacco products is prohibited to individuals under 18 years in Pakistan.
    • Governed by the Prohibition of Smoking and Protection of Non-Smokers Health Ordinance, 2002.
    • Section 8 prohibits sales to minors; Section 9 bans sale, storage, and distribution within 50 meters of educational institutions.

      Which Muslims majority area of Punjab was awarded to India by the 1947 Boundary Commission?

      This decision, along with the awarding of the Gurdaspur district to India

      83

      The Karakoram Highway starts from ______?

      Correct Answer: Hasan Abdal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Karakoram Highway (often abbreviated to KKH)

      The Karakoram Highway, also known as the Friendship Highway in China, was built by the governments of Pakistan and China

      Karakoram highway is also known as N-35

      The Karakoram Highway starts from Hassan Abdal.

      Karakoram highway was completed in 1979.

      its length in Pakistan is 1300 km.

      other related question 

      The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

      Karakoram Range is a mountain range located between China and Pakistan

      The road connecting Pakistan and China is called Karakoram Highway

      Khunjerab pass connects Pakistan with China

      84

      Wana is the main town of which Agency in FATA?

      Correct Answer: South Waziristan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Wāṇa or Wanna is the largest town of South Waziristan District in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province of Pakistan.

      South Waziristan is the largest agency of FATA.

      It is divided into three administrative subdivisions: Sarwakai, Ladha, and Lower Wana.

      85

      Reko Diq reserves of copper and gold are situated in ______ district of Baluchistan.

      Correct Answer: Chagai
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Reko Diq, a significant reserve of copper and gold.
      It is located in the Chagai district of Baluchistan.
      This site has been a focus of mining and exploration for its vast mineral wealth.
      86

      Pakistan biggest source of electricity is :

      Correct Answer: Natural source
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      According to the Electric Sector Of Pakistan:

      The largest and biggest source of electricity generation in Pakistan is Natural gas 

      The second largest and biggest source of electricity generation in Pakistan is Hydal

      Electricity generation

      Natural gas: 32.3% of the total.

      Hydroelectric: 24.7% of the total.

      Furnace oil: 14.3% of the total.

      Coal: 12.8% of the total.

      Nuclear: 8.8% of the total.

      Wind: 4.8% of the total.

      Solar:1.4% of the total.

      Bagasse: 0.9% of total.

      Quaid e Azam's tenure as Governor?

      Show explanation

      from 14 August 1947 – 11 September 1948

      He was the 1st Governor-General of Pakistan.

      Succeeded by: Khawaja Nazimuddin


      Additional information:

      Muhammad Ali Jinnah joined the league in 1913.

      He was 37 years old when he joined Muslim League.

      He joined the Indian National Congress in 1906 and left in 1920

      He was Born on December 25, 1876, in Karachi

      He died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi

      • Location: Kalabagh, Mianwali District

      The court has recalled its December 2020 orders to attach certain PIA assets in the Reko Diq case against Pakistan.

      **

      Allama Iqbal is a National Poet of Pakistan

      Allama Iqbal Died: April 21, 1938, in Lahore

      Allama Iqbal Place of burial: Tomb of Allama Iqbal, Lahore


      92

      According to the Article 18(a) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, No person shall be punished for the same offence more than _____?

      Correct Answer: Once
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Article 13(a) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 states: No person shall be prosecuted or punished for the same offence more than once.
      This is the constitutional guarantee against double jeopardy.

      Born: 13 November 1899 Murshidabad, Bengal Presidency

      Died: 13 November 1969 (aged 70) London, England

      Iskander Mirza took charge of the office on 23 March 1956.

      Iskander Mirza was the first President of Pakistan to belong to the Republican Party.

      Major Gen Sikandar Mirza was the Last Governor-General of Pakistan.

      Who abrogated the 1956 constitution? Iskander Mirza

      Which of the following Governor-General and PM of Pakistan died in exile? Iskander Mirza

      Murshidabad ( India) is the birthplace of Iskander Mirza.

      ***


      ND16-8-2023

      97

      Who laid the foundation stone of Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College?

      Correct Answer: Lord Lytton
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in 1875, initially as a primary school, with the intention of turning it into a college-level institution.
      • In 1877, the school was raised to college level.
      • Lord Lytton, the Viceroy of India at the time, laid the foundation stone of the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College (MAO College) on January 8, 1878.
      • The college was later upgraded to Aligarh Muslim University (AMU) in 1920.
      98

      The famous place Makli in Sindh is a?

      Correct Answer: Graveyard
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Makli Necropolis is one of the largest funerary sites in the world.

      spread over an area of 10 kilometers near the city of Thatta, in the Pakistani province of Sindh

      The site was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1981 as an "outstanding testament" to Sindhi civilization between the 14th and 18th centuries.

      99

      Hassan Ali Effendi established “Sindh Muhammaden Association” on the Advice of ________?

      Correct Answer: Justice Syed Ameer Ali
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Syed Ameer Ali established the London Muslim League in 1908.

      In 1909, Syed Ameer Ali became the first Indian to sit as a member of the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council on which he would serve till his death in 1928.

      100

      In 1809, the Treaty of Amristar was signed between Maharaja Ranjit Singh and the British East India Company, designating the _____ as the boundary separating the Sikh Empire in Punjab from British-controlled Indian territories.

      Correct Answer: Sutlej River
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Treaty of Amritsar (1809) established peace between Maharaja Ranjit Singh and the British East India Company.

      The Sutlej River was designated as the boundary between the Sikh Empire and British-controlled territories.

      101

      Sher Shah Suri and his successors ruled 15 years. This period is known as the period of the _____ Empire.

      Correct Answer: Second Afghan
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      First Afghan Empire: This refers to the Lodi dynasty, which ruled the Delhi Sultanate from 1451 to 1526, until they were defeated by the Mughal emperor Babur.
      Second Afghan Empire: Founded by Sher Shah Suri in 1540 after he defeated the Mughals.
      Though his own reign lasted only five years, his dynasty continued to rule until the Mughals, under Humayun, reclaimed power in 1555.
      Third Afghan Empire: This typically refers to the later Durrani Empire, founded by Ahmad Shah Durrani in 1747, which at its peak controlled parts of Central Asia and northern India.
      102

      Who gave the name famous "Kohinoor Diamond" to Emperor Humayun?

      Correct Answer: Raja of Gwalior
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The family of the Raja of Gwalior presented the famous diamond to Emperor Humayun.

      This occurred after the First Battle of Panipat in 1526.

      It following the defeat and death of Ibrahim Lodi and the Raja of Gwalior. 

      103

      Which among the following ports was called Babul Makka (Gate of Makkah) during the Mughal Period?

      Correct Answer: Surat
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Surat was known as “Babul Makkah” (Gate of Makkah) during the Mughal period.

      It served as the main port for pilgrims traveling from India to Makkah for Hajj.

      104

      The British Prime Minister Ramsay Macdonald announced the Communal Award in 1932 at the end of ______?

      Correct Answer: 2nd Round Table Conference
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The British Prime Minister Ramsay MacDonald announced the Communal Award in 1932 at the end of the Second Round Table Conference.

      The Communal Award was a series of elections for minority groups in society and the economy.

      It was established on August 16, 1932, as a result of the second round table meeting. 

      105

      British Governor-General who followed the spirited "Forward Policy" towards Afghanistan was ______?

      Correct Answer: Lytton
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Lord Lytton, who was the Governor-General of India from 1876 to 1880, followed a spirited "Forward" policy towards Afghanistan.
      • He believed that the British should pursue an aggressive policy towards Afghanistan, which led to the Second Anglo-Afghan War.
      • Lytton was appointed by Conservative Prime Minister Benjamin Disraeli and purportedly feared the spread of Russian influence in Central Asia.
      106

      Which of Allama Iqbal's Book was published after his death?

      Correct Answer: Aramghan-e-Hijaaz
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Armaghan-e-Hijaz was published posthumously in 1938, after Allama Iqbal’s death.
      It is a collection of his Persian and Urdu poetry written during his final years.
      107

      Which system of land revenue was instituted by Sir Thomas Munro, Governor of Madras, in 1820?

      Correct Answer: Ryotwari
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Ryotwari system established a direct relationship between the British government and the individual cultivators, known as "ryots".

      This eliminated the role of intermediaries, such as zamindars, in the tax collection process.

      Under this system, the cultivators were recognized as the landowners of their plots.

      As long as they paid the fixed revenue, they had the right to sell, lease, or mortgage their land. 

      108

      What type(s) of writ was/were issued by the Sindh Chief Court in the case of 'Federation of Pakistan vs. Maulvi Tamizuddin Khan'?

      Correct Answer: Both A and B
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In the Federation of Pakistan vs. Maulvi Tamizuddin Khan case (1955), the Sindh Chief Court issued writs of Mandamus and Quo-warranto.

      These writs challenged the Governor-General’s dissolution of the Constituent Assembly and questioned the legality of his actions.

      109

      Who was a prominent Punjabi folk music singer of Pakistan, credited with popularizing the musical term Jugni?

      Correct Answer: Alam Lohar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In Pakistan, Jugni was popularized by the late folk music singer Alam Lohar.

      Alam Lohar was born in 1928 in Achh, near Kotla Arab Ali Khan, Gujrat District 

      Died: 3 July 1979 (aged 51) Sham ki Bhattian

      Years active: 1936 – 1979

      Known for: Punjabi folk music, Chimta playing

      Awards: Pride of Performance Award by the President of Pakistan in 1979

      110

      "Scientific Society of Aligarh" was managed by ______?

      Correct Answer: Raja Jai Kishan Das
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Scientific Society of Aligarh was founded by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan in 1864.

      It was managed by Raja Jai Kishan Das, who supported Sir Syed’s educational and reform efforts.

      111

      The real name of Ustad Bukhari (Sindhi-language poet)?

      Correct Answer: Syed Ahmed Shah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Born: Syed Ahmed Shah Bukhari on 16 January 1930 in Dadu, Sindh (British India).

      Died: 9 October 1992 in Karachi, Pakistan.

      112

      The late Dr Ruth Pfau was granted Pakistani citizenship in _____?

      Correct Answer: 1988
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Dr. Ruth Pfau

      • Date of Birth: 9 September 1929
      • Date of Death: 10 August 2017
      • Other Name: Mother Teresa of Pakistan (due to her work for leprosy patients)
      • Dr. Ruth Pfau, was granted Pakistani citizenship in 1988.

      Major Awards:

      • Hilal-e-Imtiaz (Pakistan)
      • Hilal-e-Pakistan
      • Nishan-e-Quaid-i-Azam
      • Staufer Medal (Germany)
      • Dame of the Order of St. Gregory the Great (by Pope)
      113

      States of Dir, Chitral and Swat were incorporated in NWFP in ______?

      Correct Answer: July 1969
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The states of Dir, Chitral, and Swat were incorporated into Pakistan on July 28, 1969.

      This was announced by Yahya Khan. 

      History

      The Wāli of Swat, Miangul Abdul Wadud, acceded his state to Pakistan on November 3, 1947. 

      Chitral opted to accede to Pakistan after the partition of India in 1947. 

      The Dir state was incorporated into Pakistan on July 28, 1969, ending its political existence. 

      The royal status of the Nawabs of Dir was abolished in 1972. 

      The Swat princely state was dissolved in 1969 and incorporated into Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province.

      The North-West Frontier Province (NWFP) was renamed Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in 2010

      114

      Sindh Sagar Doab is between which two rivers?

      Correct Answer: Jhelum and Indus
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Five Important Doabs of Pakistan 

      Sindh Sagar Doab is b/w: Indus & Jhelum rivers 

      Jech Doab is b/w: Jhelum & Chenab rivers 

      Bari Doab is b/w: Ravi & Sutlej rivers

      Rachna Doab  is b/w: Ravi  & Chenab rivers  

      Bias Doab is b/w: Bias & Sutlej rivers 

      Doab Chaj is b/w: Chenab and Jhelum.



      115

      Hazrat Mahal who fought during the War of Independence 1857 against the British was?

      Correct Answer: Begum of Awadh
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Begum Hazrat Mahal, was the second wife of Nawab of Awadh Wajid Ali Shah, and the regent of Awadh in 1857–1858.

      She is known for the leading role she had in the rebellion against the British East India Company during the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

      116

      Who was the seventh prime minister of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Feroz Khan Noon
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Malik Feroz Khan Noon was the seventh Prime Minister of Pakistan, serving in this capacity from his appointment on 16 December 1957, until being removed when the President of Pakistan Iskandar Ali Mirza imposed martial law with the 8 October 1958 Pakistani coup d'état.

                                            List of Prime Ministers of Pakistan
      Term# Name Took office Left office Elections Political party
      1 Liaquat Ali Khan 14-Aug-47 16-Oct-51 — Muslim League
      2 Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin 17-Oct-51 17-Apr-53 Muslim League
      3 Mohammad Ali Bogra 17-Apr-53 12-Aug-55 Muslim League
      4 Chaudhry Mohammad Ali 12-Aug-55 12-Sep-56 Muslim League
      5 Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy 12-Sep-56 17-Oct-57 Awami League
      6 Ibrahim Ismail Chundrigar 17-Oct-57 16-Dec-57 Muslim League
      7 Sir Feroze Khan Noon 16-Dec-57 7-Oct-58 Republican Party
      8 Nurul Amin 7-Dec-71 20-Dec-71 1970 Pakistan Muslim League
      9 Zulfikar Ali Bhutto 14-Aug-73 5-Jul-77 1977 PPP
      10 Muhammad Khan Junejo 24-Mar-85 29-May-88 1985 Pakistan Muslim League
      11 Benazir Bhutto 2-Dec-88 6-Aug-90 1988 PPP
      12 Nawaz Sharif 6-Nov-90 18-Jul-93 1990 PML (N)
      13 Benazir Bhutto 19-Oct-93 5-Nov-96 1993 PPP
      14 Nawaz Sharif 17-Feb-97 12-Oct-99 1997 PML (N)
      15 Mir Zafarullah Khan Jamali 23-Nov-02 26-Jun-04 2002 PML (Q)
      16 Chaudhry Shujaat Hussain 30-Jun-04 26-Aug-04 PML (Q)
      17 Shaukat Aziz 28-Aug-04 15-Nov-07 PML (Q)
      18 Yousaf Raza Gillani 25-Mar-08 19-Jun-12 2008 PPP
      19 Raja Pervaiz Ashraf 22-Jun-12 24-Mar-13 PPP
      20 Nawaz Sharif 5-Jun-13 28-Jul-17 2013 PML (N)
      21 Shahid Khaqan Abbasi 1-Aug-17 31-May-18 PML (N)
      22 Imran Khan 18-Aug-18 Incumbent 2018 PTI
      117

      The Indo-Pakistani water dispute that emerged in ______ served as the catalyst for the eventual signing of the Indus Waters Treaty in 1960 between Pakistan and India.

      Correct Answer: April 1948
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Indo-Pakistani water dispute began in April 1948, when India stopped water flow from canals to Pakistan.
      • The Indus Waters Treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan and brokered by the World Bank.
        • It allocates the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
        • It was signed in Karachi by Pakistan President Ayub Khan, Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru, and Mr. W.A.B. Illif. 
        • The Treaty became effective on April 1, 1960.
        118

        Pakistan became a full member of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) on ____?

        Correct Answer: 9th June 2017
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • On 9 June, 2017 India and Pakistan became full members of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO).
        • The SCO Headquarters: Beijing, China
        • The SCO Founded: June 15, 2001
        • The SCO currently comprises of nine Members States (China, India, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Russia, Pakistan, Tajikistan, Iran and Uzbekistan), four Observer States
        119

        When was Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee established in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: 1976
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The post of Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee (CJCSC) was created in March 1976 by Prime Minister Zulfikar Ali Bhutto.

        The first CJCSC was General Muhammad Shariff, a four-star rank officer.

         

        120

        Who was the founder of the Azad Pakistan Party, the first opposition political party established after Pakistan's Independence?

        Correct Answer: Mian Iftikharuddin
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Mian Iftikharuddin founded the Azad Pakistan Party in 1949, making it the first opposition party after independence.

        The party aimed to promote socialist and progressive reforms in Pakistan.

        121

        Pakistan test-fired its first submarine-launched cruise missile Babur-III on 9 January 2017, has the range in kilometres?

        Correct Answer: 450 kilometres
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The range of the Babar-3 cruise missile is 450 Km.

        The ranges of other variants of the Babur cruise missile:

        Babur-1A: 450 Km

        Babur-2: 750 Km

        Babur-IB: 900 Km

        Harbah: 700 Km

        Harbah (export variant): 290 Km
        122

        Which Movement in India started in response to Shuddhi and Sangathan Movement of Hindus?

        Correct Answer: Tablighi and Tanzim Movement
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Tablighi and Tanzim Movements emerged as a Muslim response to the Hindu Shuddhi and Sangathan Movements.

        Their aim was to revive Islamic values and protect Muslims from conversion to Hinduism.

        123

        Which pass connects the Chitral with Badakhshan Afghanistan province?

        Correct Answer: Durah Pass
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Dorah Pass connects the Badakshan Province of Afghanistan with Chitral District in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

        The Dorah Pass is more than 14,000 feet (4,300 m) high.

        The Dorah Pass is located along the Durand Line border and crosses the Hindu Kush mountain range.

        *********

        IMPORTANT QUESTION RELATED TO "DISTRICT CHITRAL"

        District Chitral is the largest district in the Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa province.

        Covering an area of 14,850 km².

        Part of the Malakand Division.

        Kunar river passes through Chitral

        Shandur Pass is a pass located in District Upper Chitral. It is often called the 'Roof of the World'.

        Shandur Pass connects Chitral with Gilgit and is just 381 meters long

        Lowari Pass is a high mountain pass that connects Chitral with Dir 

        Broghil is a relatively low pass. that connects Chitral with Wahkan

        ********

        ND3-12-2022

        124

        The Khudai khidmatgar Movement was started by?

        Correct Answer: Abdul Ghaffar Khan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Founder: Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan (Bacha Khan). 

        Year of Establishment: 1929. 

        Primary Goal: To bring social reform and awaken the political consciousness of the Pashtuns. 

        Dissolved 1947

        Preceded by Roshani movement

        Succeeded by Weekh Zalmian

        125

        Shah Rafi-ud-Din was the scholar who translated the Holy Quran into ______?

        Correct Answer: Urdu
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Shah Rafi-ud-Din was among the first scholars to translate the Holy Quran into Urdu.

        He was the son of Shah Waliullah Dehlawi, a renowned Islamic scholar.

        126

        Which of the following Barrage is on River Indus? Chashma Barrage, Sukkur Barrage, Kotri Barrage, All of these

        Correct Answer: All of these
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Six barrages on the Indus River

        1. Guddu Barrage
        2. Sukkur Barrage
        3. Kotri Barrage 
        4. Taunsa Barrage
        5. Chashma Barrage
        6. Jinnah Barrage.

        The Indus River starts in Tibet

        The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

        The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

        The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

        River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

        The old name of the Indus River was: Sindhu

        National River of Pakistan: Indus River

        Indus River Length: 3,180 km

        Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

        The upper Indus Plain ends at Mithankot 

        The upper Indus Plain starts from Kalabagh

        127

        “Shams-ul-Ulama” this was awarded to ______?

        Correct Answer: Both A and B
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The title “Shams-ul-Ulama” means Sun of the Scholars.

        Mirza Qaleech Baig – Sindhi scholar and writer, awarded Shams-al-Ulama in 1924 by the British government.

        Dr. Umar bin Muhammad Daudpota – Renowned educationist and linguist, received the same title in 1945 for his literary services.

        128

        Elsa Kazi, commonly known as “Mother Elsa”, wife of Allama I.I. Kazi was born in ______?

        Correct Answer: Germany
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Elsa Kazi was born in Germany in 1884.

        She was the wife of Allama Imdad Ali Imam Kazi and is honored as “Mother Elsa” in Sindh.

        129

        The Tomb of Shah Shams is located in which city?

        Correct Answer: Multan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

         The Tomb of Shah Shams Sabzwari is located in Multan

        He established a dargah there and preached Islam to the local population

        He died in 1276 in Multan, where his mausoleum is located.

        The Urs of Shamsuddin Sabzwari takes place in June of each year.

        130

        Which of the following is the name given to Pakistani missile? Ghauri, Shamshir, Hyderi, Akbari

        Correct Answer: Ghauri
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Ghauri is the name of a Pakistani medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM).

        It is also known by the military designation Hatf-V.

        It is named after the 12th-century Sultan Shahabuddin Ghauri.

        Pakistan has developed several variants of the Ghauri missile, including the Ghauri-I and Ghauri-II, and was also working on a Ghauri-III. 

        131

        Which of the following countries is connected with Pakistan via Taftan Border?

        Correct Answer: Iran
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Taftan Border connects Pakistan with Iran in Balochistan province.

        It serves as an important trade and travel route between the two countries.

        132

        Who is the Present/Current Deputy Speaker of National Assembly of Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Ghulam Mustafa Shah
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Ghulam Mustafa Shah is the current deputy speaker of National Assembly. 

        Appointing date: 01 March 2024

        Party name: PPP

        Predecessor: Zahid Akram Durrani


        133

        Which of the following Sufi saints belong to “Chistia order”?

        Correct Answer: Farid-ud-Din Ganj Shakar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Baba Farid is a Sufi saint who belonged to the Chishti order


        The Chishti Order is a tariqa,

        It is an order within the mystic Sufi tradition of Sunni Islam.

        It is known for its emphasis on love, tolerance, and openness.

        It began in Chisht, a small town near Herat, Afghanistan, about 930 AD.

        134

        Shah Abdul Latif Bhitai was born in _____?

        Correct Answer: 1689 AD
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai, was a Sindhi Sufi mystic, and poet.

        He is widely considered to be the greatest poet of the Sindhi language. 

        Born: November 18, 1689, Hala

        Died: January 1, 1752, Bhit Shah

        135

        Article 16 of the Constitution allows citizens to assemble peacefully and without arms, subject to reasonable restrictions in the interest of _____?

        Correct Answer: Public order
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Article 16 (actually Article 16 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973) grants citizens the right to assemble peacefully and without arms.

        This right is subject to reasonable restrictions in the interest of public order.

        136

        Who demanded a separate Muslim homeland in 1930 at Allahabad?

        Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Allama Iqbal made a public proposal in Allahabad

        This address was made On 29 December 1930

        It is given during the 25th Session of the All-India Muslim League

        *****

        Allama Iqbal is a National Poet of Pakistan

        Allama Iqbal Born: November 9, 1877, Sialkot

        Allama Iqbal Died: April 21, 1938, in Lahore

        Allama Iqbal Place of burial: Tomb of Allama Iqbal, Lahore

        ND23-4-2023

        137

        When was All India Muslim League founded?

        Correct Answer: 30 December 1906
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The All India Muslim League was founded on 30 December 1906 in Dacca
        • Location: Dhaka (Dacca), Bangladesh
          • It's founder was Nawab Khwaja Salimullah.
          • It's first President was Sir Agha Khan.
          • The headquarters of the All India Muslim League was in Lucknow.
          • The party was dissolved on 14 August 1947.

          When the Muslim League ended in 1947, it split into three parties:

          1. Muslim League in Pakistan
          2. Awami League in Bangladesh
          3. Indian Union Muslim League in India

          Last updated question by NS on 11 May 2026

          138

          The Pakistan Resolution was adopted in which city?

          Correct Answer: Lahore
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The resolution was passed at Minto Park (now Iqbal Park) in Lahore.

          On March 23, A.K. Fazul Haq, the Chief Minister of Bengal, moved the historical Lahore Resolution

          ********

          Lahore Resolution Translation English to Urdu: Mulana Zafar Ali Khan

          Lahore Resolution Presented by A.K Fazul Haq        

          Participate From Punjab (Leader): Zafar Ali Khan

          Participate From Sindh (Leader): Sir Abdullah Haroon

          Participate From Balochistan (Leader): Qazi Muhammad  Isa

          Participate From KPK (Leader): Sardar Aurangzaib Khan

          CM Banglad : A.K Fazul Haq 

          United Province: Khalid Zaman

          ***

          139

          Which mountain range separate Afghanistan from Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: The Hindu Kush Range
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Hindu Kush is a vast mountain range that stretches between central Afghanistan and Northern Pakistan.
          • The Hindu Kush is 800 kilometers (500 miles) long and covers an area of roughly 152,800 square kilometers (59,000 square meters).
          140

          What does PSDP stand for?

          Correct Answer: Public Sector Development Programme
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          PSDP refers to the Public Sector Development Programme.

          It is a government initiative focused on the allocation of resources for development projects in Pakistan.

          141

          Which of the following pass connects Dir and Chitral ?

          Correct Answer: Lawari Pass
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Important Passes of Pakistan

          1. Khyber Pass 

          connects: Peshawar to Kabul

          connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

          Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


          2. Bolan Pass 

          Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

          Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


          3. Lowari Pass 

          Connects: Chitral with Dir 

          Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


          4. Broghil Pass 

          Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

          Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


          5. Malakand Pass 

          Connects: Dir with Chitral 

          Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


          6. Shandur pass

          Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

          Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


          7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

          Other name: Friendship Gate

          Connects: China with Pakistan 

          Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


          8. Dorah pass

          Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

          Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


          9. Babusar Pass 

          Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


          10. Kohat Pass

          Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


          11. Khojak Pass

          Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


          12. Tochi Pass

          Connects: Ghazni with Bannu

          Which Pass connects Chitral with Gilgit and is just 381 meters long ?

          Correct Answer: Shaundur Pass
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Important Passes of Pakistan

          1. Khyber Pass 

          connects: Peshawar to Kabul

          connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

          Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


          2. Bolan Pass 

          Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

          Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


          3. Lowari Pass 

          Connects: Chitral with Dir 

          Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


          4. Broghil Pass 

          Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

          Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


          5. Malakand Pass 

          Connects: Dir with Chitral 

          Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


          6. Shandur pass

          Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

          Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


          7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

          Other name: Friendship Gate

          Connects: China with Pakistan 

          Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


          8. Dorah pass

          Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

          Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


          9. Babusar Pass 

          Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit
