          Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


          11. Khojak Pass

          Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


          12. Tochi Pass

          Connects: Ghazni with Bannu




          143

          The first constructed barrage of Pakistan was _____?

          Correct Answer: Sukkur barrage
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Sukkur Barrage is a barrage on the River Indus near the city of Sukkur.

          The barrage was built during the British Raj from 1923 to 1932 and was named Lloyd Barrage.

          Located in Sindh Province


          AGM 02.05.2023


          144

          Who was the Abul Kalam Azad?

          Correct Answer: Indian journalist
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Maulana  Abul Kalam Azad was an Indian scholar

          He was the senior Muslim leader of the Indian National Congress

          Born: November 11, 1888, Makkah, Saudi Arabia

          Died: February 22, 1958 Delhi, India

          Education: Al-Azhar University (1905–1907)

          Organizations founded:

          • Jamia Millia Islamia,
          • Indian Council for Cultural Relations,
          • Imarat-e-Shariah,
          • Anjuman-i-Ulama-i-Bangala
          145

          Turbat is located in which province of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Balochistan
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Turbat is a major city in the Kech District of Balochistan Province.

          It is one of the largest cities in the province

          They lie in the Makran region of southwestern Pakistan.

          It is the second-largest city in Balochistan after Quetta and the 35th largest city in the country by population.

          Number of Union councils: 1

          146

          Who was the founder of Mughal Empire in India?

          Correct Answer: Babar
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Babur, the first Mughal emperor, was born in present-day Uzbekistan and became ruler of Kabul in Afghanistan.

          From there, he invaded the kingdom of the Lodi Afghans in northern India in 1526 

          There are 17 Mughal  families who rule in India.

          147

          Pakistan army won which medal in Exercise Cambrian Pat­rol-2025?

          Correct Answer: Gold medal
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          A Pakistan Army team won the gold medal at Exercise Cambrian Pat­rol-2025

          This exercise held in the United Kingdom (UK) from October 3 to 13, 2025.

           

          Additional Information:

          Pakistan also won the gold medal at the exercise in 2015, 2016, 2017 and 2024.

          ***

           

          148

          Lords of the Khyber is written by ____?

          Correct Answer: Andre Singer
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Lords of the Khyber is the Story of the North-West Frontier Hardcover.

          Published in November 1, 1984
          149

          The Indian National Congress was formed in ____?

          Correct Answer: December 28, 1885
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Indian National Congress was founded on December 28, 1885, at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay.

          Its headquarters are in New Delhi.

          Allan Octavian Hume was appointed as General Secretary.

          Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee was elected the first President.

          150

          Karakorum Highway between China and Pakistan was completed in which year?

          Correct Answer: 1979
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Karakorum highway was started in 1959 and was completed and opened to the public in 1979.

          The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

          The silk road connects Pakistan and China.

          The total length is approximately 1,300 kilometers, of which 887 kilometers are in Pakistan and 413 km in China.

          The silk road is considered a symbol of Pakistan-China's eternal friendship

          The highway meets the Pakistani-Chinese border at Khunjerab Pass

          Karakorum Highway is located in the Karakoram range

          Karakorum highway in Pakistan starts from Hassan Abdal

          Karakorum highway is also called N-35

          ****


          151

          The old name of Indus river was:

          Correct Answer: sindhu
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Indus River starts in Tibet

          The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

          The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

          The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

          River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

          The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

          National River of Pakistan: Indus River

          6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

          Indus River Length: 3,180 km

          Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

          The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 

          The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

          ND11-2-2023

          152

          Area of Thar Desert in square miles is ?

          Correct Answer: 77,000
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The total area of the Thar Desert is approximately 200,000 square kilometers (77,000 square miles). 

          About Thar Desert

          15% located in Pakistan

          85% located in India

          Also called Friendly Desert

          Located near Badin, Sindh 

          Famous for: Sand 

          Coal also found in Thar 

          also known as Great Indian Desert

          7th largest desert on the planet

          3rd largest in Asia.


          153

          Which desert in Pakistan is considered the 17th largest in the world?

          Correct Answer: Thar Desert
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The 17th largest desert in the world is the Thar Desert.

          It is also known as the Great Indian Desert.

          It is a large, arid region in the northwestern part of the Indian subcontinent. 

          The Thar Desert is situated in both India and Pakistan.

          It is with about 85% of it located in India and the rest in Pakistan.

          154

          Which battle in 1971 led to the surrender of Pakistan forces in East Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Battle of Hilli
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          The Battle of Hilli (1971) was a key engagement in East Pakistan during the Indo-Pak war.
          It contributed to the surrender of Pakistani forces and the creation of Bangladesh.
          155

          When was simla agreement signed?

          Correct Answer: 2 July 1972
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Simla Agreement was signed on July 2, 1972.

          In between Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and Pakistani President Zulfikar Ali Bhutto.

          It established the Line of Control (LoC) in Kashmir and facilitated the repatriation of prisoners of war and the return of occupied territories.

          156

          Quaid-e-Azam made his first speech at All India Radio on ______?

          Correct Answer: 3rd June 1947
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The founder of Pakistan had delivered the speeches on June 3 and August 14, 1947.

          These were recorded by All India Radio in Delhi because there was no recording facility in Karachi, Lahore or Peshawar stations at that time.

          157

          What is the old name of Balochistan Capital (Quetta)?

          Correct Answer: Shalkot
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The capital of Balochistan is Quetta also known as Shalkot.

          The city is also known as the “Fruit Garden of Pakistan” as the large variety of fruits and dry fruits produced there.

          It is a trade and communication centre between Pakistan and Afghanistan borders.

          158

          On which river is Head Qadriabad is located?

          Correct Answer: Chenab
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The headworks of Qadirabad are located on the Chenab River.

          This barrage was completed in 1967.

          It is used for irrigation and flood control in the Punjab province of Pakistan. 

          Water is also diverted from the Rasul Barrage on the Jhelum River to the Qadirabad headworks via the Rasul-Qadirabad link canal.

          159

          The last town on the Karakorum Highway in Pakistan before the Chinese border is _______?

          Correct Answer: Sost
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Sost or Sust (سوست‎) is a village in Gojal, Upper Hunza, Gilgit–Baltistan, Pakistan.

          It is the last town inside Pakistan on the Karakoram Highway before the Chinese border.

          It is elevated 2,800 meters above sea level.

          160

          Who wrote "Muslim League Yesterday and Today"?

          Correct Answer: A.B Rajput
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The book Muslim League: Yesterday & Today was written by A.B. Rajput.

          The first edition was published in 1948 in Lahore. 

          The book covers the history and politics of the All-India Muslim League and Pakistan.

          161

          Second highest civil award of Pakistan is ______?

          Correct Answer: Hilal-i-Imtiaz (Hilal-e-Pakistan)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hilal-i-Imtiaz: civilian award and honour given to both civilians and military officers of the Pakistan Armed Forces by the Government of Pakistan.

          Nishan-e-Pakistan: highest civil Award 

          Tamgha-e- Khidmat: Smallest Civil Award 

          Highest military's award is Nishan-e-Haider

          Captain Raja Muhammad Sarwar, the country's first recipient of Nishan-i-Haider,

          • RK04-12-2022



          162

          When Sir Syed Ahmad Khan was transferred to Aligarh, after the construction of the building for Scientific Society?

          Correct Answer: 1864
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          On January 9, 1864 Sir Syed Ahmad Khan formed a translation society called Scientific Society at Ghazipur.

          163

          Which Pakistani Fast bowler got the ICC Test Cape of the year 2018?

          Correct Answer: Muhammad Abbas
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Muhammad Abbas was named the ICC Men's Test Cricketer of the Year for 2018, recognizing his outstanding performance in Test cricket during that period.

          He was also included in the ICC Men's Test Team of the Year for 2018.

          164

          Princess of Hope is in _______?

          Correct Answer: Lasbela (Balochistan)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Princess of Hope is a natural rock formation in Lasbela, Balochistan, shaped over years by the Arabian Sea breeze.

          The rock resembles a princess looking beyond the horizon, appearing like a masterpiece of a skilled artisan.

          The name “Princess of Hope” was given by Hollywood actress Angelina Jolie during her visit.

          165

          The “Treaty of Friendship” was signed in 1951 between?

          Correct Answer: Pakistan and Saudi Arabia
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Treaty of Friendship. January 04, 1950

          On February 14, 1950, the Governments of the Soviet Union and the People's Republic of China signed a 30-year: Treaty of Friendship,

          Alliance,

          and Mutual Assistance which was ratified by both sides and entered into force on April 11, 1950.

          ND14-7-2023

          166

          Who was the first female lieutenant general of Pakistan Army?

          Correct Answer: Nigar Johar
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Major General Nigar Johar, has been promoted as Lieutenant General.

          She is the 1st female officer to be promoted as Lieutenant General.

          The officer has been appointed as 1st female Surgeon General of Pak Army.

          Lieutenant General Nigar Johar hails from Panjpeer, District Swabi KPK.


          نگار جوہر پاکستان آرمی کی پہلی خاتون لیفٹیننٹ جنرل 2020میں بن گئیں۔

          167

          Quaid-e-Azam gave his Fourteen Points in response to ____?

          Correct Answer: Nehru report
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Jinnah gave his points in order to safeguard and protect the rights and interests of Muslims in March 1929.

          Muhammad Ali Johar helped Quaid e Azam in preparation for fourteen points 

          Nehru report presented in 1928

          Jinnah’s Fourteen Points presented in 1929

          1.      Federal constitution with residual powers with the provinces.

          2.      Provincial autonomy.

          3.      No constitutional amendment without the agreement of the states.

          4.      All legislatures and elected bodies to have adequate Muslim representation without reducing Muslim majority in a province to minority or equality.

          5.      Adequate Muslim representation of Muslims in the services and in self-governing bodies.

          6.      1/3rd representation of Muslims in the Central Legislature.

          7.      1/3rd Muslim members in the central and state cabinets.

          8.      Separate electorates.

          9.      No bill to be passed in any legislature if 3/4th of a minority community considers it against its interests.

          10.   Any reorganisation of territories not to affect the Muslim majority in Bengal, Punjab and the NWFP.

          11.   Separation of Sindh from Bombay Presidency.

          12.   Constitutional reforms in the NWFP and Baluchistan.

          13.   Full religious freedom for all communities.

          14.   Protection of the religious, cultural, educational and language rights of Muslims.

          ND23-4-2023



          168

          Who was first lady member of Balochistan Assembly?

          Correct Answer: Fazeela Alyani
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Fazila Aliani (born 1945) is a veteran politician, women's rights activist and educationist from Balochistan, Pakistan.

          She was the first woman minister of Balochistan when she was appointed Minister of Health, Education and Social Welfare in 1976.

          She was also the first woman elected to the Balochistan Assembly in 1972.

          169

          Who has become first ever woman Deputy Speaker of Balochistan Assembly?

          Correct Answer: Ghazala Gola
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Ghazala Gola made history by becoming the first woman ever elected Deputy Speaker of the Balochistan Assembly

          Oath taking date: 29 Feb 2024

          Party name: PPP

          170

          Durand line is between _____?

          Correct Answer: Pak-Afganistan
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The length of the common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan new is 2611 km old was 2430km

          ...

          The common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is called Durand Line

          ..

          British signed the Treaty of Durand line with Abur Rehman Khan

          The agreement was signed on November 12, 1893, in Kabul, Afghanistan.

          The agreement was signed between Sir Mortimer Durand, the Indian Foreign Secretary at the time and Amir Abdur Rahman Khan in Kabul.


          ********

          According to the Survey of Pakistan, the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km

          ND18-2-2023

          ND23-4-2023

          171

          Who was the founder of Scientific Society?

          Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Sir Syed Ahmed Khan founded Scientific Society in 1864.

          Sir Syed Ahmed Khan established a Translation Society (later, renamed as ‘Scientific society’).

          In which town was it founded Ghazipur.

          172

          PIDC stands for what _____?

          Correct Answer: Pakistan Industrial Development
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          PIDC stands for Pakistan Industrial Development Corporation

          PIDC is a state corporation of Pakistan working under the Ministry of Industries and Production.

          It was established in 1952. 

          PIDC's Subsidiary: Pakistan Gems and Jewellery Development Company

          ND29-5-2023


          173

          Name the range which separates Pakistan from Afghanistan?

          Correct Answer: Hindu Kush
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Hindu Kush mountain range separates parts of Pakistan and Afghanistan.

          Hindu Kush's Highest point, Naw Shakh
          Passes           Kotal-e Salang, Wakhjir Pass, Gomal Pass, Dorah Pass

          The Hindu Kush is an 800-kilometre-long mountain range that stretches through Afghanistan, from its centre to Northern Pakistan and into Tajikistan.

          Who is the National poet of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          National Poet of Pakistan is Allama Iqbal

          About Allama Iqbal

          Allama Iqbal Born: November 9, 1877, Sialkot

          Allama Iqbal Died: April 21, 1938, Lahore

          Allama Iqbal Children: Javed Iqbal, Aftab Iqbal, Muneera Bano, Miraj Begum

          Allama Iqbal Spouse: Sardar Begum

          When did Rashid Minhas got Nishan-e-Haider?

          Correct Answer: 1971
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Rashid Minhas received the Nishan-e-Haider in 1971.
          • He received this honor posthumously for his actions in preventing a traitor from stealing a fighter plane on August 20, 1971.
          176

          Allama Iqbal did his Bar at Law from ______?

          Correct Answer: Lincoln's Inn University
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Allama Iqbal was awarded his law degree (bar-at-law) from Lincoln's Inn in London.

          He also earned a Ph.D. in philosophy from the University of Munich in 1908.

          177

          Captain Muhammad Sarwar was martyred in the war of _____?

          Correct Answer: 1948
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Captain Muhammad Sarwar Cause of Death: Indo-Pakistani War of 1947–48.

          Captain Raja Muhammad Sarwar Bhatti NH, BS, best known as Muhammad Sarwar, was an officer in the Pakistan Army

          was cited with the first Nishan-i-Haider during the first war between India and Pakistan in 1947–48.

          Raja Muhammad Sarwar Bhatti was born in a small village, Singhori, Gujar Khan, Rawalpindi District, on 10 November 1910,

          Captain Muhammad Sarwar  Died on 27 July 1948 (aged 37) in Uri, Jammu and Kashmir in India

          Captain Muhammad Sarwar was Buried at the Hill of Tilpatra near Uri, Jammu, and Kashmir in India

          agm.04.03.23

          ND17-7-2023


          178

          PILDAT stands for?

          Correct Answer: Pakistan Institute of Legislative Development and Transparency
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Pakistan Institute of Legislative Development and Transparency,

          It is a Pakistani think tank that promotes democracy.

          They also organize the Youth Parliament of Pakistan.

          Formation: 2001

          Founder: Ahmed Bilal Mehboob

          Headquarters: Pakistan

          179

          What is the length of Durand line?

          Correct Answer: 2611 Km
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Durand Line is the 2,611-kilometer (1,622-mile) border between Afghanistan and Pakistan

          The common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is called Durand Line

          British signed the Treaty of Durand line with Abur Rehman Khan

          The agreement was signed on November 12, 1893, in Kabul, Afghanistan.

          The agreement was signed between Sir Mortimer Durand, the Indian Foreign Secretary at the time, and Amir Abdur Rahman Khan in Kabul.

          ********

          According to Survey of Pakistan 2020, the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km.

          check here: www.surveyofpakistan.gov.pk

          We often heard Durand Line length is 2252 KM. But at most of the places, we read 2430 KM.

          but right answer is 2611


          180

          Which hospital in Kasur city was renamed as Bulleh Shah Hospital?

          Correct Answer: The District Headquarter (DHQ) Hospital
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          There is a DHQ hospital in Kasur city, which has been named as Bulleh Shah Hospital. 

          181

          Kasur is located in which division?

          Correct Answer: Lahore Division
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Kasur District is a district within the Lahore Division of Punjab

          It was established on 1 July 1976.

          Before its creation, the area comprised two tehsils (Kasur Tehsil and Chunian Tehsil)

          Kasur city, the birth city of the Sufi poet Bulleh Shah

          182

          SNGPL stands for?

          Correct Answer: Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          SNGPL stands for Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited

          It is a Pakistani state-owned gas distribution company 

          It is listed on the Pakistan Stock Exchange.

          Serving more than 7.22 million consumers in north central Pakistan

          Founded: 1963

          Headquarters: Lahore

          Area served: Punjab, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Azad Kashmir & Islamabad

          The company has 16 distribution regions.

          183

          NLC stands for?

          Correct Answer: National Logistics Corporation
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          NLC can stand for National Logistics Corporation, 

          It was formerly known as the National Logistics Cell,

          It is a Pakistani state-owned logistics company based in Rawalpindi,

          Founded July 20, 1978; 47 years ago

          Headquarters Rawalpindi,

          184

          The book The Spread of Islam in the World is written by:

          Correct Answer: Sir Thomas W. Arnoid
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Spread Of Islam In The World by Thomas Walker Arnold.

          The book, which was first published in 1896

          Thomas Walker Arnold CIE FBA was a British orientalist and historian of Islamic art.

          He taught at Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College, later Aligarh Muslim University, and Government College University, Lahore



          185

          Babri mosque which was demolished in 1992 was situated in the province in _____?

          Correct Answer: Utar Pardesh
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Babri Masjid is located in Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh, India

          On 6 December 1992, a large group of Hindu activists of the Vishva Hindu Parishad and allied organizations demolished.

          Babri Masjid Completed in 1528–29

          Babri Masjid Demolished in 1992





          186

          Who wrote the book Yadgar-i-Ghalib?

          Correct Answer: Altaf Husain Hali
          Show explanation
          Explanation

           Yadgar-i-Ghalib is a 1897 biography of the Urdu poet Ghalib,

          He wwas ritten by his disciple and writer/poet Altaf Hussain Hali (1837–1914).

          Publication date 1897

          It is considered to be the first authentic work on Ghalib's life, personality, poetry and prose.

          187

          Khawaja Altaf Hussain Hali was awarded the title of Shamsul Ulama.

          Correct Answer: 1904
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          In 1904, Hali was given the title of Shamsul Ulama by the British Government.

          This title was reserved for the highly learned.

          He was an Urdu poet and writer.

          Years active 1860–1914

          Notable works Musaddas-e-Hali

          • Yadgar-i-Ghalib
          • Hayat-i-Saadi
          • Hayat-i-Javed
          188

          Number of members of election commission ?

          Correct Answer: 4
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Election Commission of Pakistan have 4 members panel from all the four provinces .

          4 Provincial Election Commissioners.

          The Commission transacts its business by holding meetings.

          The Chief Election Commissioner have tenure of 5 years.

          The members Election Commissioner have tenure of 5 years.



          189

          APNEC stands for?

          Correct Answer: All Pakistan Newspapers and Electronic Media Employees Confederation
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          APNEC stands for the All Pakistan Newspapers and Electronic Media Employees Confederation.

          It was established in 1974 to protect the rights of both journalistic and non-journalistic staff 

          190

          PICIC stands for_____?

          Correct Answer: Pakistan International Commerce and Investment Council
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          PICIC stands for Pakistan Industrial Credit and Investment Corporation

          It is a financial institution in Pakistan,

          It is one of the first development finance institutions established with the World Bank Group

          Founded: 1957; 68 years ago

          Headquarters Pakistan

          191

          WASA stands for ________?

          Correct Answer: Water and Sanitation Agency
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          WASA stands for Water and Sanitation Agency

          It was established by the Lahore Development Authority (LDA) in 1976

          For the planning, designing, development, and maintenance of water supply, sewerage, and drainage systems in Lahore.

          192

          All India National Congress was formed/established by _____?

          Correct Answer: A.O Hume
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Indian National Congress was founded on 28 December 1885 

          It was founded at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay 

          Its Headquarters is located in New Delhi

          It was founded by Allan Octavian Hume.


          Additional information

          Allan Octavian Hume Place was appointed as the General Secretary,

          Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee of Calcutta was elected the first President.


          193

          Who took oath from PM Liaqat Ali Khan ?

          Correct Answer: Quaid-i-Azam
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Liaqat Ali Khan was named Pakistan’s first Prime Minister by the country’s founding fathers after the country gained independence in 1947.

          Liaqat Ali Khan administered the oath to Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

          ****

          194

          Chief of Army Staff General Asim Munir has been promoted to which rank in May 2025?

          Correct Answer: Field Marshal
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          On 20 May 2025, the federal cabinet approved the promotion of Chief of Army Staff (COAS) General Asim Munir to Field Marshal.

          For ensuring the security of the country and defeating the enemy based on the high strategy and courageous leadership of the Battle of Truth, Operation Bunyan-um-Marsoos


          Additional Information:

          The rank of Field Marshal is the highest rank of armies built on the patron of the British Army.

          It may be noted that after his promotion, Gen Asim Munir will become the country’s second field marshal only after Field Marshal Ayub Khan.

          195

          Muslim League was founded in which year?

          Correct Answer: 1906
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The All India Muslim League was founded on 30 December 1906 in Dacca, British India (now Dhaka, Bangladesh).
            • It's founder was Nawab Khwaja Salimullah.
            • It's first President was Sir Agha Khan.
            • The headquarters of the All India Muslim League was in Lucknow.
            • The party was dissolved on 14 August 1947.

            When the Muslim League ended in 1947, it split into three parties:

            1. Muslim League in Pakistan
            2. Awami League in Bangladesh
            3. Indian Union Muslim League in India
            196

            Who was the Defence Minister of Pakistan in 2016?

            Correct Answer: Khawaja Muhammad Asif
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Khawaja Asif served as the Defense Minister of Pakistan in 2016,

            Holding the position from November 2013 to July 2017

            During Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif's third government. 

            197

            Badshahi Masjid in Lahore is also called

            Correct Answer: Emperor mosque
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Badshahi Mosque, or the 'Emperor's Mosque', is a mosque in Pakistan.

            It was built in 1673 by the Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb in Lahore, Pakistan, near the Minar-e-Pakistan.


            198

            What was the magnitude of the Earthquake that shook northern Pakistan And Azad kashmir on Oct 8, 2005?

            Correct Answer: 7.6
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The earthquake of October 2005 was result of Tectonic dislocation

            The 2005 Kashmir earthquake occurred at 08:50:39 Pakistan Standard Time on 8 October in Pakistani-administered Azad Kashmir.

            Magnitude: 7.6 Mw

             More than 80,000 people perished as a result of the quake,

            while an estimated 4 million others were left homeless

            The Picture on the back of 1000 Rupee Pakistani Note?

            Correct Answer: Islamia College Peshawar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Picture on the back of 1000 Rupee Pakistani Note Islamia College Peshawar


            • The first banknotes issued by the State Bank of Pakistan were released on the 1st of October 1948, i printed by Thomas De La Rue & Company.
            • ***
            • The Picture on the back of the 100 Rupee Pakistani Note Quaid-e-Azam Residency
            • The Picture on the back of the 500 Rupee Pakistani Note Badshahi Mosque
            • The Picture on the back of the 10 Rupee Pakistani Note is Khyber Pass
            • The Picture on the back of the 5000 Rupee Pakistani Note Faisal Mosque 

            ***

            200

            The city of textiles is__________?

            Correct Answer: Faisalabad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The city of textiles is Faisalabad

            It was formerly known as Lyallpur

            It is famous for its textile mills

            It is the third-largest city in Pakistan

            It is the second-largest city in the Province of Punjab.

            It is called the Manchester of Pakistan because it's the biggest textile city in Pakistan

            Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow for the next lesson.
